ko: 나는 컨설팅 탐정이야, 이 직업을 발명한 유일한 사람이야.

en: The only one in the world that invented the job. What does that mean?
It means when the police are out of their depth, which is always, they consult me.
ko: 경찰이 힘들어할 때, 항상 그렇지, 나에게 상담해.

en: When I met you for the first time yesterday, I said Afghanistan or Iraq.
ko: 어제 처음 만났을 때, 아프가니스탄이나 이라크라고 했더니 놀란 표정을 지었지.

en: You looked surprised. Yes, how did you know? I didn't know, I saw.
The haircut, the way you hold yourself, says military. But your conversation as you entered the room...
ko: 네 머리 스타일과 자세가 군인 같아, 하지만 방에 들어올 때의 대화는...

en: A bit different from my day. Said trained at Bart, so army doctor, obvious.
Your face is tanned. But no tan above the wrists.
ko: 얼굴은 그을렸지만, 손목 위는 태닝이 없어.

en: You've been abroad, but not sunbathing.
Your lip's really bad when you walk, but you don't ask for a chair when you stand, like you've forgotten about it.
ko: 걸을 때 절뚝거림이 심한데, 서 있을 때 의자 요청은 안 해, 마치 잊어버린 것처럼.

en: So it's at least partly psychosomatic. That says the original circumstances of the injury were traumatic.
ko: 그러니까 최소한 부분적으로 심리적 원인이 있어.

en: Wounded in action, then. Wounded in action, suntan, Afghanistan or Iraq.
ko: 전투 중 부상당했군, 전투 중 부상, 태닝, 아프가니스탄이나 이라크.

en: You said I had a therapist. You've got a psychosomatic lip, of course you've got a therapist.
And there's your brother. Your phone, it's expensive.
Email-enabled MP3 player. Are you looking for a flat share? You wouldn't waste money on this.
ko: 너는 원룸을 찾고 있어, 이런 데 돈 낭비하지 않을 거야.
